inputs.userGoal: A serverless app with Functions and Cosmos DB
subGoals: Cost-optimized: consumption/serverless tiers, single region, minimal complexity
Security-first: managed identity for service-to-service auth, Key Vault for secrets, RBAC authorization
Experiment/reference architecture — not expuction-grade (zone redundancy, VNet/private endpoints omitted)

resources.name: rg-maxsales-exp-eastus

reasoning.whyChosen: Single resource group for all resources simplifies management for this experiment. All services share the same lifecycle.
alternativesConsidered: Separate resource groups per service tier
reasoning.tradeoffs: Single RG means all resources share the same lifecycle and IAM boundary. Acceptable for experiment; expuction should split by lifecycle.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Resource group has no dependencies — it is the container.

resources.name: stmaxsalesexpeastus

reasoning.whyChosen: Required by Azure Functions (consumption plan) for code storage and internal queue management. StorageV2 is the required kind — BlobStorage/FileStorage lack Queue/Table support needed by Functions. LRS chosen for cost optimization.
reasoning.tradeoffs: LRS is cheapest but no zone or geo redundancy. Functions consumption plan cannot use VNet-secured storage anyway, so networking hardening is limited by the Functions plan choice.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Storage account is deployed into the resource group.

resources.name: id-maxsales-exp-eastus

reasoning.whyChosen: User-assigned managed identity for Function App to authenticate to Cosmos DB, Key Vault, and Storage without connection strings or keys. Enables passwordless authentication.
reasoning.tradeoffs: User-assigned is preferred — reusable across resources, survives resource deletion, and enables CMK at resource creation. Slightly more complex to set up than system-assigned.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Managed identity is deployed into the resource group.

resources.name: kv-maxsales-exp-eus

reasoning.whyChosen: Stores connection strings, API keys, and Cosmos DB credentials as secrets. RBAC authorization (not access policies) for consistent IAM model. Soft-delete protects against accidental deletion.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard SKU enough for experiment. Purge protection disabled to allow cleanup. Premium HSM not needed for this workload. Network defaults to Allow for simplicity — expuction should restrict to private endpoint.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Key Vault is deployed into the resource group.

resources.name: log-maxsales-exp-eastus

reasoning.whyChosen: Centralized log aggregation for Function App, Cosmos DB, and Key Vault diagnostic settings. PerGB2018 tier for pay-per-use pricing. 30-day retention sufficient for experiment.
reasoning.tradeoffs: PerGB2018 costs scale with log volume — monitor costs. Free tier available but only 7-day retention limits troubleshooting window.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Log Analytics workspace is deployed into the resource group.

resources.name: appi-maxsales-exp-eastus

reasoning.whyChosen: Workspace-based Application Insights for Function App telemetry — request tracing, exceptions, dependencies, and custom metrics. Required by WAF guidance for observability.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Workspace-based is the recommended modern approach. Classic is being phased out. Log Analytics retention governs data lifecycle.

resources.dependencyReasoning: App Insights depends on Log Analytics workspace for data storage (workspace-based mode). Both must be in the same resource group.

resources.name: cosmos-maxsales-exp-eastus

reasoning.whyChosen: Cosmos DB serverless for cost-efficient NoSQL storage with consumption-based pricing — only pay for RUs consumed. Session consistency balances performance and cost. disableLocalAuth enforces RBAC-only access via managed identity.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless: single-region only, no multi-region writes, 50GB max storage per container, no analytical store. Not suitable for high-throughput workloads or geo-distribution. disableLocalAuth means all access must use Entra ID (managed identity) — no key-based access.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Cosmos DB is deployed into the resource group. If using CMK: requires Key Vault, but serverless does not support CMK.

resources.name: func-maxsales-exp-eastus

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure Functions Consumption plan — true serverless, scales to zero, pay-per-execution. Node.js runtime matching existing project. HTTPS-only enforced. Managed identity for passwordless auth to Cosmos DB and Key Vault.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Consumption: cold starts on idle, 10-min max execution, no VNet integration, no VNet-secured storage. Flex Consumption would add VNet and longer execution but at higher cost. Premium adds VNet, private endpoints, Always Ready — overkill for experiment.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Storage account required for Functions runtime. Managed identity for auth to Cosmos DB and Key Vault. Key Vault for Application Insights connection string. Cosmos DB for data access. App Insights for telemetry.

overallReasoning.summary: Fully serverless architecture: Azure Functions Consumption for compute, Cosmos DB serverless for NoSQL data, with Storage Account for Functions runtime. Managed identity provides passwordless auth between all services. Application Insights + Log Analytics for observability. Key Vault for secrets. Single region (eastus), minimal cost — services scale to zero when idle.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Single region = no DR/HA beyond Azure's platform guarantees. No VNet/private endpoints = public endpoints (tradeoff for cost and simplicity; documented in subGoals). No purge protection on Key Vault (cleanup-friendly). Cosmos DB serverless limits: 50GB/container max, no geo-replication, no analytical store, burstable throughput only. Functions Consumption: cold starts, 10-min max execution, no VNet integration.

plan.validation: All resources deployable in eastus region. No SKU compatibility conflicts detected. Serverless Cosmos DB + Consumption Functions are compatible pairings. StorageV2 meets Functions requirements. User-assigned managed identity supports all target services. App Insights workspace-based configuration compatible with Log Analytics.
architecturePrinciples: Serverless-first — no VMs, no clusters, pay-per-execution
Identity-driven security — managed identity, RBAC, no keys/connection strings in code
Observable — Application Insights + Log Analytics for all telemetry
Cost-optimized — consumption/serverless tiers, single region, no premium networking
